message: this prompt asks for a quality judgement on AI output — run eval-gate: score it against a WRITTEN rubric (0-5, exit-code gate) rather than eyeballing. Below the line, no ship; every caught failure becomes a permanent case. Write the rubric as a file first if none exists.

claim_pattern: (?i)\b(?:draft|post|answer|reply|copy|content|email|message|article|blog ?post|response|write-?up)\b[^.?!\n]{0,45}\b(?:is (?:ready|final|good to go|done|solid)|ready to (?:ship|post|publish|send)|good to go|looks good|ship it|send it|publish it)\b
message: about to ship AI-generated CONTENT to an audience without gating its quality — run eval-gate (score against the rubric) before it leaves the building, or say it is shipping ungated. Slop is an output-side problem; the score is the layer that measures it.

you appear to be WEAKENING a gate to make it pass — don't. A tolerance / threshold / expectation change needs explicit human sign-off, never mid-run to convert FAIL→PASS. Raise the bar, never lower it to wave work through.

pattern: (?i)\b(?:what do you think|which (?:do you|one do you) prefer|prefer\b|judge (?:this|it|these)|feedback on (?:this|these|it|the \w+)|which (?:one|version|option)?\s*(?:passes|wins|is better|looks best|looks better)|review this|feels off|favorite|thoughts on)\b
unless_pattern: (?i)(?:\b(?:scoring guide|acceptance|done means|pass if|threshold|must have|measuring|success means|judge it by)\b|\bcriteri(?:a|on)\b[^.\n]{0,10}[:=](?!\s*(?:TBD|TBA|none|n/?a|later|pending|\?+)\b)|\brubric\b[^.\n]{0,12}[:=](?!\s*(?:TBD|TBA|none|n/?a|later|pending|\?+)\b))
message: asking the user to judge/review an artifact with no stated criterion nearby — live incident: 'here's the compare board — which do you prefer?' gave the user nothing to measure against. Broadened to catch review this/feels off/looks best/prefer/favorite/thoughts-on asks too. A bare mention of the word 'criteria'/'criterion'/'rubric' (e.g. a file name, or 'no criteria yet', or 'rubric file is attached for naming only') does NOT suppress this — only an actual stated criterion (scoring guide:, acceptance:, done means:, pass if, threshold, must have, or 'criteria/criterion:'/'rubric:' followed by a definition) does. A definition-shape match (criteria:/rubric: followed by a colon or equals) does NOT suppress when the value right after it is a placeholder ('TBD', 'TBA', 'none', 'n/a', 'later', 'pending', or bare '?'/'??') — so 'rubric: TBD; which wins?' and 'criteria: none yet; which looks best?' still fire; a placeholder is not a stated criterion. State the judging criterion (measuring X, success means Y, judge it by Z) before or alongside the ask, or run eval-gate against a written rubric instead of outsourcing the grading to eyeballing.
_note: ADVISORY heuristic over unbounded natural language — observed-incident phrasings + common variants are covered and regression-tested (3 adversarial rounds); paraphrase false-negatives and topic-collision false-positives remain possible by design and are accepted (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3: heuristic banked with scope of validity).
